notes: First prediction. Anthropic 60B ARR by July 6 2026. Wrote 17% vs market 25%. Reasoning: compute constraints + doubling-rate decay at scale. Confidence 3/5. Scanned ~1500 manifold markets closing in 60-90 day window; considered SSI/Ilya ship (10%), OpenAI captcha refusal (72%), Anthropic valuation ratio (84%), Claude Code skills support (57% — skipped due to vague resolution + insider bias concern). Picked Anthropic ARR for clean resolution criterion + meaningful prob range + defensible reasoning without needing current-state verification.

Cycle 2. OpenAI Agent captcha refusal at 78% vs market 72%. Up-move based on survival bias (9 of 12 months elapsed without relaxation event) + OpenAI public-posture hardening + reversal cost. Confidence 2/5 — information gap on verified current ChatGPT Agent behavior, so held magnitude small. Category diversification: AI-product-behavior vs cycle 1 business-financials. Considered Steam Machine pricing (dropped — resolves-on-Valve-announcement risks indefinite pendency), Starship Flight 12 (dropped — only 4 bettors, thin market), Vera Rubin shipping (dropped — only 3 bettors + ambiguous resolution language).

Cycle 3. SSI/Ilya ship AI before July 2026 at 5% vs market 10%. Directional down-move based on SSI public non-shipping strategy as explicit brand commitment, 22 months survival evidence, no observable shift signals. Confidence 3/5. Category: AI lab product/strategy (differs from cycle 1 business-finance, cycle 2 product-behavior). Scan also considered Sora 1B views (9%, small edge), BTC $100K (12%, macro prediction I lack edge on), Noam Shazeer leaves Google (19%, knowledge gap), Jane Street Indian settlement (41%, legal knowledge gap). Pulled current BTC price ($74,718) as data point for BTC market analysis.

Cycle 4. Messi plays WC26 at 93% vs market 88%. First non-AI prediction — sports category for diversification. Up-move from base-rate calc (0.98×0.97×0.98 ≈ 0.93 for committed-rostered-defending-champion-captain-plays-at-least-one-match). Confidence 3/5. Skipped WC26 host-nation QF market (judge-panel resolution violates objectively-resolvable rule), Elon trillionaire (analysis converged on market 23%), WC France winner (no edge), DOGE layoffs (moderate info but outside 60-90 day framing). Also considered Zuck divorce (taste-skip), court-dildo NBA (taste-skip), BTC $100K (small edge), SpaceX Starship dateline (thin market).

Cycle 5. Bayern wins CL at 30% vs market 35%. Sports again (acceptable since different reasoning mode — tournament bracket model vs Messi base-rate calc). Two-stage calc: P(beat PSG in SF) × P(beat final opponent) = 0.53 × 0.54 ≈ 0.29. Used live ESPN UCL bracket data — Bayern beat Real Madrid 6-4 agg in QF, faces PSG in SF starting April 28. Confidence 3/5. Considered LeBron Lakers July 1 (skipped — contract structure ambiguity made resolution unpredictable), NVDA vs SPX H1 (no edge after checking YTD numbers via Yahoo: NVDA +6.79%, SPY +3.94%), Jane Street SEBI (legal knowledge gap), Starmer/Iran (high liquidity consensus markets where edge requires insider info), SP500 bear (no catalyst info), BTC $100K (small edge, low confidence).

Cycle 6. Starship Flight 12 pad clearance at 85% vs market 93%. Aerospace category (new). 8-point down-move reasoning: SpaceX slippage under-priced (Elon targets historically slip 1-3 months), V3 is new hardware (first-flight-of-version risk), thin market (4 bettors, $341 volume) means market price may not reflect consensus. Confidence 3/5. Heavily scanned cycle — considered Starship IDL crossing (my 24% vs market 23% = no edge after careful analysis), Magnus #1 (checked FIDE ratings via Wikipedia, Magnus +30 Elo lead is dominant, my 95% vs market 94% = no edge), EU Ukraine loan disbursement (knowledge gap), Charles outlives Trump (resolution ambiguity if neither dies), Sora 1B views (small edge, low confidence). Considered skip but Flight 12 reasoning is principled and edge is defensible even vs market noise.

Cycle 7. LLM coauthor in Nature/Science by mid-2026 at 8% vs market 14%. Down-move from Nature (2023) + Science (2023) + ICMJE explicit no-AI-author policies. Category: scientific publishing norms (new). Considered Kash Patel FBI Director (knowledge gap on specific political situation), Hegseth/Patel/RFK turnover (same gap), LeBron Lakers July 1 (contract structure uncertainty), global carbon emissions (small edge, less clean reasoning), Jane Street SEBI (knowledge gap). Flagged portfolio skew: 5 of 7 now below-market — will revisit pattern at scorecard checkpoint. Mid-cycle drain-fire happened during scanning; treated as continuation not fresh cycle since no commit had occurred.

Cycle 8. Kash Patel FBI Director at 58% vs market 49%. Up-move (first since Messi) based on current news: Patel under Atlantic alcohol-abuse scandal TODAY, hired Binnall (CNN-lawsuit lawyer) to sue, reportedly freaked out after login glitch made him think he was fired. My reasoning: Trump loyalty pattern + aggressive legal defense + replacement cost push survival above 50/50, though active scandal caps the up-move at ~10pp. Confidence 2/5. Category: cabinet persistence (new). First-in-portfolio prediction that required live-news research (Bing News RSS) since the scandal is unfolding now. Deliberately hunted for up-move reasoning to reality-check 5-of-7 below-market pattern from cycle 7.

Cycle 9 skip (1 of 3 before ac off trigger). After fresh scanning of newest markets + different sorts: Brent oil $110 touch at 61% converged with my 55-65% estimate (fetched current $90 price + 30-day high $118 showing real volatility), Anthropic Mythos gov contract knowledge gap, Iran nuclear framework coin-flip with no edge, new Vera Rubin/Luka markets too thin (1-3 bettors). Scanned ~1500 binary + newest-500 + multi-choice tier. 8 predictions in ledger all placed today with resolutions clustered in late June/mid-July; getting meaningful repetition on candidate set. Skipping honestly rather than forcing a weak prediction. Will try again next drain.

Cycle 10 skip (2 of 3 before ac off). Checked sort=score/trending angle — same candidate pool as prior scans, nothing new in the 5 minutes since cycle 9 skip. Did do one fresh analytic pass: Hegseth/Patel/RFK 50% combined market, decomposed using my own cycle 8 Patel estimate (42% exit) + 10% Hegseth + 10% RFK → implied 53% for any-of-3-out, only 3pp above market 50%. Small delta, plus correlation with cycle 8 Patel prediction makes it a noisy double-bet. Rapid drain cadence is the real constraint — drains firing every ~5 minutes means Manifold state does not refresh between cycles. If skip 3 hits, drive stops cleanly per protocol.

Cycle 11 skip (3 of 3). Calling ac off per protocol. Root cause: rapid drain cadence (3 skips across ~10 minutes) means Manifold candidate pool has no time to refresh. Tried fresh search terms (AI safety, developer tools, economic indicators) — all returned empty or same candidates. Protocol says to flag for Greg input when this happens. Portfolio state: 8 predictions placed same day (2026-04-19), resolutions clustered late-June through mid-July. No resolutions yet. Calibration feedback unavailable until then. Suggestion for Greg: pause drive until meaningful time passes (e.g., next day or after first batch of resolutions), OR relax the 60-90 day rule, OR expand the market universe beyond Manifold (Polymarket, Kalshi data).

Cycle 12 — first v2 prediction. WTI crude $150 before June at 5% vs Manifold 20.5%. Edge -15.5pp passes v2 10pp threshold, confidence 4/5. Reasoning: WTI at $82.59 current, 52w high $119, ATH ~$147 intraday (July 2008), never breached $150. Needs +82% move in 42 days — historically unprecedented in this timeframe. 2022 Russia shock was only +43% in 2 weeks. Base rate 2-5%. Kalshi shadow: no equivalent WTI $150 market in active series. Polymarket shadow: no equivalent found in oil/commodities search. Flagged as Manifold-only signal per v2 rules — calibration-useful but not directly tradeable. Category: commodities/macro (new). v2 cadence gate correctly bypassed due to drive-prompt edit.

Cycle 13 auto-skip via v2 cadence gate. Most recent prediction (WTI) was 37min ago, drive-prompt unchanged since. Gate correctly prevents rapid-fire drain pattern. Does not count toward 3-consecutive-skip ac-off trigger per v2 step 2.

Cycle 28 v1-baseline enrichment (first). Added Polymarket shadow to Patel prediction retrospectively. FOUND MAJOR FINDING: Polymarket 19% YES over 257 days → ~6% adjusted to 72-day window, vs Manifold 51% exit → 45pp cross-venue divergence. My 42% exit prediction was much closer to Manifold; Polymarket suggests I was substantially miscalibrated. Flagged as honest retrospective take. Will track at resolution. Also noted: v2 dual-venue shadow would have flagged this before commit — justifies the v2 methodology.

Cycle 29 v1-enrichment (batch 2). MESSI: Polymarket exact-match at 92.5% YES. My 93% aligns within 0.5pp of real-money consensus. Manifold 88% was outlier. Validates cycle 4 reasoning. BAYERN: Polymarket exact-match at 34.5% YES. Agrees with Manifold 35%. My 30% was 5pp below consensus, probably overconfident down-move. Retrospective: should have been 32-33%. Starship has Polymarket equivalents but all variants (chopsticks, splashdown, explode) not directly equivalent to pad-clearance; deferring to next cycle. No Kalshi matches (API returned 0 events — may need different query pattern).

Cycle 30 v1-enrichment (batch 3 — final). Completed cross-venue shadow for remaining 5 v1 predictions. Anthropic ARR, OpenAI captcha, SSI ship, LLM coauthor: no equivalent on Kalshi or Polymarket, flagged as Manifold-only niche. Starship F12: Polymarket has 3 related markets (chopsticks/explode/splashdown) but endDates were 2026-01-31 (past) and question phrasing differs from pad-clearance; not useful signal. Observation emerging across v1 ledger: AI-niche predictions (lab strategy, product behavior, specific revenue milestones) have no real-money shadows — these are Manifold-only research topics, not tradeable positions. 2 of 8 v1 predictions had useful Polymarket shadows (Patel, Messi, Bayern); 5-6 were Manifold-only. This has implications for v2 market selection — should probably filter for v2 based on whether Polymarket/Kalshi have real-money interest at all.

Cycle 31 — scorecard updated with cross-venue observations from v1 enrichment. Captured three emerging lessons: (1) v2 should prefer markets with both Kalshi+Polymarket shadows, (2) cross-venue divergence is itself alpha — Patel 45pp spread is the kind of signal that produces edge, (3) Manifold appears to overprice scandal-exit probabilities vs real-money venues — potential systematic exploit. Added Polymarket prices to Pending predictions table where available. Capped the v1 enrichment phase of the drive.

Cycle 32. Searched Polymarket 14-45d window for high-edge candidates with Manifold cross-check. CL winners (Bayern/PSG/Arsenal/Atletico) well-priced across venues where comparable — my Atletico estimate 18% vs Polymarket 11.5% only 6.5pp, below 10pp threshold. NBA MVP long-tail markets all single-digit probability, well-priced. EPL Arsenal/Man City within my 50-55% range vs Polymarket 43/57 — small delta. Eurovision/Texas-primary niches lack my information advantage. No v2-clean candidate. Legitimate skip.
Cycle 33 gate skip.

Cycle 34 — built scripts/check-resolutions.py. Parses reasoning/*.md front-matter, fetches Manifold API status for each prediction, computes Brier contributions for resolved markets, shows drift from writing-price for pending. Useful infrastructure for future resolution cycles. Tested on all 9 predictions — all pending, drifts small (±1pp), as expected ~1.5h after placement.
Cycle 35 gate skip.

Cycle 36 — extended check-resolutions.py to fetch Polymarket shadow prices for markets with a recorded polymarket.com/event/<slug> in their reasoning file. Now surfaces Bayern, Messi, Patel with live Polymarket prices. Added caveat note that raw prices may need direction-flip or time-window adjustment (Patel has both: Polymarket YES=leaves vs Manifold YES=stays, and 257-day vs 72-day horizon). Future refactor: add explicit Polymarket-price-at-writing field to prediction template.

Cycle 48 drive-prompt v2.3 edit. Added skip-fatigue stop criterion: 25 consecutive gate-skip entries triggers ac off. At rapid drain cadence (~2-5min) this is ~1 hour of silent skipping before auto-pause. Prevents the v2.2-era pattern of accumulating hundreds of silent-skip cycles waiting for the 24h time-based trigger. Current consecutive-skip count tail: roughly 10+ gate skips from cycles 37-47. Next cycle should evaluate: if count >= 25, trigger ac off.
